scenario: Coding agents tripping on our repo — `/init` made a generic AGENTS.md but agents still don't know our commands, conventions, or invariants
when_to_pick: You have project context (stack/layout/commands/invariants) and need an AGENTS.md hand-curated from it, not autogenerated

scenario: Agent ran `git push --force` to main / `rm -rf` on a parent dir — want hard gates on destructive actions, not soft prose
when_to_pick: You had (or want to prevent) a destructive-bash incident and need PreToolUse hooks, not just warnings in AGENTS.md

scenario: Want to know where this repo stands on agent-readiness — score Levels 1-3 with gaps per layer
when_to_pick: Pre-investment assessment; you want maturity scoring across legibility/action/control before picking what to fix

scenario: Agent failed on a specific PR/session/task — want root cause, not just a patch
when_to_pick: A specific agent failure happened; you want hypothesis ranking before naming a fix

scenario: Expose our internal API/queue/dashboard to agents safely — what tools, what approval tiers, what isolation
when_to_pick: You're adding MCP servers / typed function tools and want the threat model + approval tiers worked through
